t_19663889576960797663646192832892906925.readability_explanation: Clear visual hierarchy with an Overview followed by well-labeled sections and consistent bulleting makes the summary easy to scan. Formatting is uniform and key points are surfaced in bullets under each section. The introductory paragraph and the PYT section’s lead-in are somewhat dense, but the bullets quickly break down details.
t_19663889576960797663646192832892906925.fluency_explanation: Writing is grammatically sound with precise, professional wording and natural flow. Minor issues include some repetition and slight wordiness (e.g., “provided an update on their ongoing work to plan a potential Film Festival,” repeating “Film Festival” and using an elongated construction). A few phrases are slightly clunky (e.g., “ensuring accurate titles were used”), but overall fluency is strong.
t_19663889576960797663646192832892906925.coherence_explanation: Content is logically ordered, mirroring a typical agenda: minutes, subcommittee update, major review, then announcements and next steps. Each section opens with a concise summary followed by specific points, providing context before details. Transitions are smooth with related items grouped together and no noticeable jumps.
t_19663889576960797663646192832892906925.tone_explanation: The tone is consistently professional and objective, describing discussions and actions without judgment or emotive language. It avoids casual phrasing and maintains a uniform level of formality throughout. The summary is appropriate for stakeholder review and balanced in its presentation of viewpoints.
t_19663889576960797663646192832892906925.accuracy_explanation: {"overall": {"score": 0.435, "total_claims": 23, "accurate_claims": 10, "inaccurate_claims": 13, "summary": "Several claims accurately reflect the meeting\u2019s minutes approvals, film festival subcommittee plans, PYT updates, and scheduled next meeting. A number of claims add timing, recipient, or policy details not stated in GT, change certainty, or contain naming errors, leading to unsupported or factual inaccuracies."}, "claims": [{"claim_id": "c_001", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_001_p_001", "t_001_p_002", "d_001", "d_002"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT states the minutes were approved with the correction identifying Kumar as Vice Chair instead of committee member."}, {"claim_id": "c_002", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_001_p_001"], "issue_type": "unsupported_by_gt", "explanation": "GT notes Magner will work with the City Clerk and bring back a revised version, but does not specify it will be presented at the next meeting."}, {"claim_id": "c_003", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_001_p_002"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT confirms McAlister abstained because he had not attended the prior meeting."}, {"claim_id": "c_004", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_001_p_002"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT states McAlister made the motion and Kumar seconded."}, {"claim_id": "c_005", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_002_p_002", "d_003"], "issue_type": "unsupported_by_gt", "explanation": "GT assigns Yvonne to prepare a detailed budget for the committee\u2019s review; it does not specify that this is for the subcommittee."}, {"claim_id": "c_006", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_002_p_001"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT states the subcommittee would return with a full report at the next PAC meeting."}, {"claim_id": "c_007", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_002_p_001"], "issue_type": "fabricated_facts", "explanation": "GT mentions an approximate timeline but does not specify the year 2023."}, {"claim_id": "c_008", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_003_p_003", "t_003_p_004", "d_004"], "issue_type": "unsupported_by_gt", "explanation": "GT commits to revising the financial spreadsheet format and bringing updated versions; it does not commit to including overhead or broader budget context."}, {"claim_id": "c_009", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_004_p_002"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT says the seven Stories on Stage school titles were completely sold out to schools."}, {"claim_id": "c_010", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_005_p_002"], "issue_type": "changed_certainty", "explanation": "GT identifies a new state Performing Arts Grant Program as a funding source but does not assert that grants have been received."}, {"claim_id": "c_011", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_004_p_004"], "issue_type": "fabricated_facts", "explanation": "GT mentions masking policies (recommended for audiences, required for rehearsals) but says nothing about vaccination requirements."}, {"claim_id": "c_012", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_004_p_004"], "issue_type": "unsupported_by_gt", "explanation": "GT contains no reference to parent feedback on masking or vaccination policies."}, {"claim_id": "c_013", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_005_p_003"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT states rebuilding Stories on Stage audiences is a major challenge."}, {"claim_id": "c_014", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_005_p_003"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT notes rebuilding technical and seasonal staffing is a challenge, implying shortages."}, {"claim_id": "c_015", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_005_p_003"], "issue_type": "unsupported_by_gt", "explanation": "GT mentions documenting volunteer labor for sustainability, not balancing volunteer labor with paid staff."}, {"claim_id": "c_016", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_007_p_002", "t_001_p_001"], "issue_type": "factual_error", "explanation": "GT identifies Carol Donahue as Chair and shows she adjourned the meeting; Kumar is the Vice Chair, not the chair."}, {"claim_id": "c_017", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_004_p_001", "t_004_p_002", "t_004_p_003", "t_004_p_004", "t_004_p_005"], "issue_type": "unsupported_by_gt", "explanation": "GT provides Connor Day\u2019s updates but does not state his title."}, {"claim_id": "c_018", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_007_p_002"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT refers to Carol Donahue as Chair."}, {"claim_id": "c_019", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_006_p_004"], "issue_type": "unsupported_by_gt", "explanation": "GT only mentions Yvonne will confirm the City Council agenda timing; it does not state she will accept the proclamation on behalf of the committee."}, {"claim_id": "c_020", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_006_p_002", "t_006_p_003"], "issue_type": "unsupported_by_gt", "explanation": "GT notes WAA is likely more relevant but does not assign Yvonne to research that conference specifically."}, {"claim_id": "c_021", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_006_p_003", "d_005"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT and the decision assign Yvonne to gather preliminary booking information from agents on available artists and fee ranges."}, {"claim_id": "c_022", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_006_p_002"], "issue_type": "factual_error", "explanation": "GT references APAP; the claim names a 'PAP' conference, which is an incorrect conference name."}, {"claim_id": "c_023", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_007_p_001"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT confirms the November 16, 2022 meeting date and that TheatreWorks and Upstage will present."}]}
t_19663889576960797663646192832892906925.completeness_explanation: {"overall": {"score": 0.743, "total_covered_items": 23, "detail_distribution": {"rich": 7, "adequate": 7, "sparse": 8, "barebone": 1}, "summary": "Overall, the candidate preserves the core meaning of most covered items, with rich detail on minutes corrections/approval, film festival planning/budget commitments, and booking exploration. However, the PYT financial and program sections often lose important specifics (dollar amounts, counts, show-by-show outcomes, and precise policy details). Staff proclamation details were slightly altered, and next-meeting specifics (time/modality) and adjournment time were not provided."}, "covered_items": {"topics": [{"topic": "Minutes correction and approval", "points": [{"id": "t_001_p_001", "text": "Noelle Magner said the prior minutes should identify Sukanya Kumar as Vice Chair rather than committee member and said she would work with the City Clerk to bring back a revised version.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Captures that the title should be Vice Chair, Noelle will coordinate with the City Clerk, and a revised version will return next meeting."}, {"id": "t_001_p_002", "text": "After confirming there was no public comment, the committee approved the prior minutes with the Vice Chair correction. John McAlister made the motion, Sukanya Kumar seconded, Kumar and Yao voted yes, and McAlister abstained because he had not attended the earlier meeting.", "detail_score": 0.88, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Includes motion by McAlister, second by Kumar, approval, and McAlister\u2019s abstention with reason. Omits confirmation of no public comment and the specific yes votes by Kumar and Yao."}]}, {"topic": "Film Festival subcommittee status", "points": [{"id": "t_002_p_001", "text": "Sukanya Kumar said the Film Festival subcommittee had met with Chair Donahue and Theresa Yvonne, had an approximate timeline in mind, and was still working through dates and scope before returning with a full report at the next PAC meeting.", "detail_score": 0.92, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Reports meetings with Chair and staff, an approximate timeline in development, ongoing work on dates/scope, and that a full report will come at the next meeting. Adds a 2023 framing but retains core meaning."}, {"id": "t_002_p_002", "text": "Theresa Yvonne said she would prepare a detailed budget showing what the first successful film festival cost and estimated costs for the proposed new event so the committee could judge next steps.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Clearly states Theresa will prepare a detailed budget comparing past and proposed costs to inform next steps."}]}, {"topic": "PYT financial review", "points": [{"id": "t_003_p_001", "text": "Theresa Yvonne clarified that the $73,816 total included a $9,910 facility use fee paid by patrons, and that Peninsula Youth Theatre itself paid $63,906.88 in rent and facility fees.", "detail_score": 0.55, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "Mentions explaining facility fees but omits all specific dollar amounts and the key distinction that patrons paid a portion. Also introduces \u201cnet revenue,\u201d which is not in GT."}, {"id": "t_003_p_002", "text": "Magner explained that the spreadsheet abbreviations referred to venue spaces such as mainstage, second stage, park stage, and rehearsal stage, and that the listed codes represented different charge categories used by the city.", "detail_score": 0.88, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Conveys that Noelle clarified spreadsheet abbreviations and charge categories but does not enumerate the specific venue spaces or codes."}, {"id": "t_003_p_003", "text": "Staff said the spreadsheet did not include broader overhead such as utilities and janitorial costs, while some labor charges such as the house manager were billed according to the city's fee schedule.", "detail_score": 0.75, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Captures that overhead costs were excluded; omits examples (utilities, janitorial) and the detail that some labor (e.g., house manager) is billed per city fee schedule."}, {"id": "t_003_p_004", "text": "John Marchant said he and Theresa Yvonne would revise the financial document to look more like a typical spreadsheet and would bring updated versions for future home company reviews, including an updated PYT version.", "detail_score": 0.6, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "Reflects a general commitment to provide more comprehensive reports in future, but misattributes the request to McAlister and omits that Marchant and Yvonne would specifically revise the document format and bring updated versions."}]}, {"topic": "PYT programs and recovery", "points": [{"id": "t_004_p_001", "text": "Connor Day said COVID continued to affect PYT outreach, but in-person classes and rehearsals had returned and the School Play in a Box program restarted at about eight schools last season and at Bub Elementary plus 14 other schools this fall.", "detail_score": 0.6, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "Notes COVID impacts and mentions outreach programs like School Play in a Box, but omits the quantitative details (about eight schools; Bub Elementary + 14) and the explicit return of in-person classes and rehearsals."}, {"id": "t_004_p_002", "text": "Day said the current year's seven Stories on Stage school titles were completely sold out to schools, with participation from Mountain View and nearby cities including Cupertino, Sunnyvale, and Los Altos.", "detail_score": 0.75, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Conveys sold-out school performances for Stories on Stage but omits the count of seven titles and the list of participating cities."}, {"id": "t_004_p_003", "text": "PYT's Theatre in the Park camps fully returned in summer 2022 and served about 600 campers, roughly 80% of 2019 attendance and above staff's expectations.", "detail_score": 0.55, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "Mentions summer camps generally but misses key specifics: 2022 return, ~600 campers, and the 80% of 2019 benchmark above expectations."}, {"id": "t_004_p_004", "text": "For audiences, masks were recommended but not required, while masks were still required for rehearsals in the studio.", "detail_score": 0.6, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "Indicates masking policies were discussed and that some requirements remained, but does not capture the specific distinction (recommended for audiences, required for rehearsals) and introduces vaccination, which GT did not mention."}, {"id": "t_004_p_005", "text": "Day said mainstage attendance had recovered strongly: Descendants drew the biggest post-pandemic crowds until Frozen surpassed it; school shows for All Together Now and Descendants were canceled because of low interest, Fiddler held two student group performances, and SpongeBob had already sold 791 student group seats.", "detail_score": 0.5, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "References mainstage musicals and audience rebuilding at a high level but omits all show-by-show specifics (which shows peaked, cancellations, two student performances for Fiddler, and SpongeBob\u2019s 791 seats)."}]}, {"topic": "PYT funding and challenges", "points": [{"id": "t_005_p_001", "text": "Day said the committee had been given PYT's completed 2019 and 2020 Form 990s, that 2021 taxes were still being finalized, that both PPP loans had been forgiven, and that an unused SBA loan of about $300,000 was still being held as reserve while being repaid slowly.", "detail_score": 0.52, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "Touches on financials broadly but omits all key specifics about 990s, 2021 status, PPP forgiveness, and the ~$300k SBA reserve being repaid slowly."}, {"id": "t_005_p_002", "text": "Day said PYT had continued grant support from organizations including Kiwanis and Packard, and identified the state Performing Arts Grant Program as a new funding source.", "detail_score": 0.72, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Conveys continued grant support and a new state grant program but omits specific funders (Kiwanis, Packard)."}, {"id": "t_005_p_003", "text": "Day said the biggest challenges for the coming year were rebuilding Stories on Stage audiences and rebuilding technical and seasonal staffing after pandemic disruptions, while also documenting volunteer labor to judge long-term sustainability.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Captures the main challenges (rebuilding audiences, technical staffing) and references volunteer labor considerations, though it frames it as balancing paid vs. volunteer rather than documenting for sustainability."}]}, {"topic": "Booking exploration and staff updates", "points": [{"id": "t_006_p_001", "text": "John McAlister asked that the committee begin exploring how the center might book outside acts and requested background information on major industry events and available artists as PAC considers the center's future direction.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Preserves the request to explore booking outside acts and seek background on major industry events and artists to inform future direction."}, {"id": "t_006_p_002", "text": "Theresa Yvonne said the main conferences were APAP in New York and Western Arts Alliance in the western region, and said WAA was likely the more relevant event for the center's size.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Identifies both conferences and notes WAA as the more suitable one; minor naming typo (\u201cPAP\u201d) but core meaning intact."}, {"id": "t_006_p_003", "text": "Yvonne said she could contact agents to learn which artists were touring and what their fee ranges looked like, and bring that preliminary information back to the committee.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Accurately reflects her offer to contact agents, assess touring acts and fees, and report back."}, {"id": "t_006_p_004", "text": "Yvonne announced that the National Arts and Humanities Proclamation would go to City Council the following Tuesday and said she would confirm the agenda timing and send it to the group.", "detail_score": 0.68, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "Conveys that a proclamation will go to City Council, but alters details by saying she will accept it on behalf of the committee and omits the timing (following Tuesday) and the commitment to confirm/send agenda timing."}, {"id": "t_006_p_005", "text": "Carol Donahue thanked the city for the recent volunteer appreciation event and said it had been a useful opportunity to meet members of other committees and think about collaboration opportunities.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Preserves both the thanks for the volunteer event and the note about inter-committee networking/collaboration opportunities."}]}, {"topic": "Next meeting and adjournment", "points": [{"id": "t_007_p_001", "text": "The next Performing Arts Committee meeting was scheduled for November 16, 2022 at 6:15 PM by video conference, with additional home company presentations including TheatreWorks and Upstage.", "detail_score": 0.75, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Captures the date and presenting companies but omits the specific time (6:15 PM) and that it would be by video conference; also minor spelling variant for TheatreWorks."}, {"id": "t_007_p_002", "text": "Chair Donahue adjourned the October 19, 2022 meeting at 7:14 PM.", "detail_score": 0.4, "detail_level": "barebone", "assessment": "Only implies the meeting concluded; does not provide the adjournment time or explicitly state adjournment by the chair."}]}], "decisions": []}}

t_19663889576960797663646192832892906925.completeness_explanation: {"overall": {"score": 0.789, "total_covered_items": 22, "detail_distribution": {"rich": 1, "adequate": 18, "sparse": 2, "barebone": 1}, "summary": "The candidate summary demonstrates strong completeness overall, preserving the core operational meaning and important details for most covered items. The summary effectively captures key actors, timelines, decisions, and outcomes across all major topics. While some secondary details and specific numbers are compressed or omitted, the essential information needed for understanding the meeting's substance is well-preserved. A few items show minor detail loss in areas like specific vote counts or exact financial figures, but the operational significance remains clear throughout."}, "covered_items": {"topics": [{"topic": "Minutes correction and approval", "points": [{"id": "t_001_p_001", "text": "Noelle Magner said the prior minutes should identify Sukanya Kumar as Vice Chair rather than committee member and said she would work with the City Clerk to bring back a revised version.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate preserves the core correction needed (Vice Chair vs committee member) and Magner's role in fixing it. The specific mention of working with City Clerk is captured as 'coordinate with the City Clerk's office.' Minor rephrasing but all essential meaning retained."}, {"id": "t_001_p_002", "text": "After confirming there was no public comment, the committee approved the prior minutes with the Vice Chair correction. John McAlister made the motion, Sukanya Kumar seconded, Kumar and Yao voted yes, and McAlister abstained because he had not attended the earlier meeting.", "detail_score": 0.75, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the motion, second, and McAlister's abstention with reason. However, it omits the specific vote breakdown (Kumar and Yao voted yes) and the public comment confirmation. The procedural essence is preserved but some voting detail is lost."}]}, {"topic": "Film Festival subcommittee status", "points": [{"id": "t_002_p_001", "text": "Sukanya Kumar said the Film Festival subcommittee had met with Chair Donahue and Theresa Yvonne, had an approximate timeline in mind, and was still working through dates and scope before returning with a full report at the next PAC meeting.", "detail_score": 0.88, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate effectively captures Kumar's report, meetings with Chair Donahue and staff, timeline development, and the plan to return with a full report. The mention of 'working through dates and scope' is preserved as ongoing planning efforts. Very good detail preservation."}, {"id": "t_002_p_002", "text": "Theresa Yvonne said she would prepare a detailed budget showing what the first successful film festival cost and estimated costs for the proposed new event so the committee could judge next steps.", "detail_score": 0.82, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures Yvonne's commitment to prepare a detailed budget and references past festival costs for comparison. The specific purpose 'so the committee could judge next steps' is implied in 'to inform future decisions.' Good preservation of the key commitment and rationale."}]}, {"topic": "PYT financial review", "points": [{"id": "t_003_p_001", "text": "Theresa Yvonne clarified that the $73,816 total included a $9,910 facility use fee paid by patrons, and that Peninsula Youth Theatre itself paid $63,906.88 in rent and facility fees.", "detail_score": 0.65, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "The candidate mentions Yvonne explaining 'net revenue and facility fees paid by PYT' but omits the specific dollar amounts ($73,816 total, $9,910 patron fees, $63,906.88 PYT payment). The concept is preserved but important financial specifics are lost."}, {"id": "t_003_p_002", "text": "Magner explained that the spreadsheet abbreviations referred to venue spaces such as mainstage, second stage, park stage, and rehearsal stage, and that the listed codes represented different charge categories used by the city.", "detail_score": 0.78, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures Magner's explanation of spreadsheet abbreviations and charges related to facility use. The specific venue examples (mainstage, second stage, etc.) are not listed, but the core explanation of abbreviations and charge categories is preserved."}, {"id": "t_003_p_003", "text": "Staff said the spreadsheet did not include broader overhead such as utilities and janitorial costs, while some labor charges such as the house manager were billed according to the city's fee schedule.", "detail_score": 0.72, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the key point about overhead costs not being included and references the city's fee schedule. The specific examples (utilities, janitorial, house manager) are compressed to 'overhead costs' but the essential limitation of the financial data is preserved."}, {"id": "t_003_p_004", "text": "John Marchant said he and Theresa Yvonne would revise the financial document to look more like a typical spreadsheet and would bring updated versions for future home company reviews, including an updated PYT version.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the commitment to improve financial reporting and provide more comprehensive reports for future meetings. The specific mention of 'typical spreadsheet' format and future home company reviews is well preserved as 'more comprehensive financial reports' and 'future meetings.'"}]}, {"topic": "PYT programs and recovery", "points": [{"id": "t_004_p_001", "text": "Connor Day said COVID continued to affect PYT outreach, but in-person classes and rehearsals had returned and the School Play in a Box program restarted at about eight schools last season and at Bub Elementary plus 14 other schools this fall.", "detail_score": 0.75, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures COVID impacts, program restart, and school participation. The specific numbers (8 schools last season, Bub Elementary plus 14 this fall) are compressed but the growth trend and program recovery are preserved."}, {"id": "t_004_p_002", "text": "Day said the current year's seven Stories on Stage school titles were completely sold out to schools, with participation from Mountain View and nearby cities including Cupertino, Sunnyvale, and Los Altos.", "detail_score": 0.82, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the sold-out status and multi-city participation. The specific number 'seven titles' is omitted but the success (sold-out) and geographic reach are well preserved."}, {"id": "t_004_p_003", "text": "PYT's Theatre in the Park camps fully returned in summer 2022 and served about 600 campers, roughly 80% of 2019 attendance and above staff's expectations.", "detail_score": 0.7, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate mentions summer camps returning and serving campers but omits the specific numbers (600 campers, 80% of 2019) and the comparison to expectations. The program recovery is captured but important context about scale and success is lost."}, {"id": "t_004_p_004", "text": "For audiences, masks were recommended but not required, while masks were still required for rehearsals in the studio.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the masking policy distinction between audiences and rehearsals. The specific details (recommended vs required, studio rehearsals) are preserved in the discussion of 'masking and vaccination policies' with good accuracy."}, {"id": "t_004_p_005", "text": "Day said mainstage attendance had recovered strongly: Descendants drew the biggest post-pandemic crowds until Frozen surpassed it; school shows for All Together Now and Descendants were canceled because of low interest, Fiddler held two student group performances, and SpongeBob had already sold 791 student group seats.", "detail_score": 0.68, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "The candidate mentions 'mainstage musicals' and strong recovery but omits most specific show details, attendance figures (791 SpongeBob seats), and the progression from Descendants to Frozen. The recovery concept is preserved but important specifics about individual shows are lost."}]}, {"topic": "PYT funding and challenges", "points": [{"id": "t_005_p_002", "text": "Day said PYT had continued grant support from organizations including Kiwanis and Packard, and identified the state Performing Arts Grant Program as a new funding source.", "detail_score": 0.88, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures both continued grant support and new state grants. The specific organizations (Kiwanis, Packard) are preserved as examples, and the new state funding source is clearly mentioned. Excellent detail preservation."}, {"id": "t_005_p_003", "text": "Day said the biggest challenges for the coming year were rebuilding Stories on Stage audiences and rebuilding technical and seasonal staffing after pandemic disruptions, while also documenting volunteer labor to judge long-term sustainability.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the key challenges: audience rebuilding, staffing shortages, and volunteer labor documentation. The specific mention of 'Stories on Stage audiences' and 'technical and seasonal staffing' is well preserved, along with the sustainability concern."}]}, {"topic": "Booking exploration and staff updates", "points": [{"id": "t_006_p_001", "text": "John McAlister asked that the committee begin exploring how the center might book outside acts and requested background information on major industry events and available artists as PAC considers the center's future direction.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate excellently captures McAlister's request to explore booking outside acts, the need for industry conference information, and the connection to the center's future direction. All key elements are preserved with good detail."}, {"id": "t_006_p_002", "text": "Theresa Yvonne said the main conferences were APAP in New York and Western Arts Alliance in the western region, and said WAA was likely the more relevant event for the center's size.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures both conferences (APAP and Western Arts Alliance) with their locations and Yvonne's recommendation about WAA being more appropriate. The specific reasoning about center size is preserved as 'more appropriate.'"}, {"id": "t_006_p_003", "text": "Yvonne said she could contact agents to learn which artists were touring and what their fee ranges looked like, and bring that preliminary information back to the committee.", "detail_score": 0.88, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures Yvonne's offer to research artists, fees, and report back. The specific elements (contacting agents, touring artists, fee ranges) are preserved as 'research and report back on' available acts and fees."}, {"id": "t_006_p_004", "text": "Yvonne announced that the National Arts and Humanities Proclamation would go to City Council the following Tuesday and said she would confirm the agenda timing and send it to the group.", "detail_score": 0.82, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the proclamation announcement and City Council presentation. The specific timing ('following Tuesday') and agenda confirmation details are compressed but the key announcement and Yvonne's role are preserved."}, {"id": "t_006_p_005", "text": "Carol Donahue thanked the city for the recent volunteer appreciation event and said it had been a useful opportunity to meet members of other committees and think about collaboration opportunities.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures Donahue's thanks for the volunteer event and the networking/collaboration benefits. The specific mention of meeting other committee members and collaboration opportunities is well preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Next meeting and adjournment", "points": [{"id": "t_007_p_001", "text": "The next Performing Arts Committee meeting was scheduled for November 16, 2022 at 6:15 PM by video conference, with additional home company presentations including TheatreWorks and Upstage.", "detail_score": 0.78, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the November 16, 2022 date and mentions TheatreWorks and Upstage presentations. The specific time (6:15 PM) and video conference format are omitted, but the essential scheduling information is preserved."}, {"id": "t_007_p_002", "text": "Chair Donahue adjourned the October 19, 2022 meeting at 7:14 PM.", "detail_score": 0.45, "detail_level": "barebone", "assessment": "The candidate mentions meeting conclusion but omits the specific adjournment details (Chair Donahue, October 19, 2022, 7:14 PM). Only the general concept of meeting ending is preserved without the formal procedural details."}]}], "decisions": []}}
